summary: Marked melee/gm/gmopening.c as Matching and adjusted small-data/BSS symbol layout metadata and gmopening .sbss declarations to resolve a full-link SDA offset mismatch involving gm_804D67EC.

key_files.role: Changed gmopening.c from NonMatching to Matching in the GALE01 matching object list.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py hunk around MatchingFor, Object(NonMatching, "melee/gm/gmopening.c") -> Object(Matching, "melee/gm/gmopening.c")

key_files.role: Reordered/changed .sbss declarations for gm_804D67EC, gm_804D67F0, and gm_804D67F4; gm_804D67F4 changed from static to non-static.
evidence_refs: pr_body: gm_804D67F4 needed to be non-static for compiler/linker layout

key_files.role: Adjusted symbol metadata across .data, .bss, .sdata, and .sdata2, including local-label/scope/name changes and added BSS/data labels.

what_changed.text: gmopening.c was linked by changing its configure.py object entry from NonMatching to Matching.
The gmopening .sbss declarations were rearranged so gm_804D67EC is declared after gm_804D67F0 and gm_804D67F4 in source order while retaining address comments, and gm_804D67F4 became non-static.
evidence_refs: pr_body: gm_804D67EC now lands at expected offset; gm_804D67F0 and gm_804D67F4 follow in right order; gm_804D67F4 needed non-static

what_changed.text: symbols.txt changed several previously global address-style symbols to local @NNN labels and added local section labels such as ...data.0 and ...bss.0.
The PR body attributes the issue to an external SDA reference from fn_801A1498 to gm_804D67EC resolving to the right symbol name but wrong final small-data offset after gmopening was marked matching.
The bot report showed linked code +2164 bytes and linked data +744 bytes for GALE01, with no listed changes beyond the report summary.

smart_moves.text: The fix targeted the owning translation unit's small-data layout rather than compensating at the external reference site.
evidence_refs: pr_body: gm_804D67EC is owned by gmopening.c; fix was to make gmopening’s .sbss layout match the target

smart_moves.text: The PR paired the matching-status flip with symbol metadata changes, acknowledging that full-link matching depends on section layout and symbol scope, not only function text.
evidence_refs: pr_body: data symbol size/final padding and .sbss layout discussion

decomp_lessons.lesson: Before marking a translation unit Matching, check final link layout for SDA-owned symbols that are referenced from other objects; the object can be text-matching while still shifting a small-data offset used elsewhere.
evidence_refs: pr_body: external SDA reference to gm_804D67EC from fn_801A1498 loaded right symbol name at wrong final offset
raw_diff: gmopening.c marked Matching in configure.py

decomp_lessons.lesson: For small-data layout fixes, source declaration order and storage class can be layout-affecting; in this PR gm_804D67F4 had to be non-static and ordered with gm_804D67EC/gm_804D67F0 for the linker layout to line up.
evidence_refs: pr_body: gm_804D67F4 needed to be non-static

decomp_lessons.lesson: Symbol metadata scope/name corrections can be part of matching a linked object; local @NNN labels and section labels may better represent compiler-owned literals or section starts than global address-style symbols.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt changes from gm_/it_/ftKb_Init global names to local @NNN labels and ...data.0/...bss.0 labels

naming_conventions.name: gm_804D67EC, gm_804D67F0, gm_804D67F4
naming_conventions.convention: Address-style names were retained for gmopening .sbss variables where semantic names were not provided by evidence.
Several literals/constants were represented as local compiler-style @NNN labels rather than global address-style names.

naming_conventions.name: unused_floats
naming_conventions.convention: A local float block at .data:0x803DBF78 was renamed from address-style gm_803DBF78 to a descriptive local name.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use full-link/SDA offset symptoms to identify data-layout mismatches in the owning translation unit.
evidence_refs: pr_body: external SDA reference to gm_804D67EC and wrong final small-data offset

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjust .sbss declaration order and static/global visibility to match target small-data placement.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gmopening.c reordered gm_804D67F4/gm_804D67F0/gm_804D67EC and removed static from gm_804D67F4
pr_body: gm_804D67F4 needed non-static for compiler/linker layout

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Update symbols.txt section metadata, scope, and labels to reflect expected data/sdata/sdata2/bss ownership and alignment.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR number/title/state/author/timestamps and changed files
human_pr_text: author explanation of full-link mismatch and intended fix
text_corpus: bot report with linked code/data deltas and no review comments
notes: There were no review comments and no human review approvals in the loaded slice.
The loaded evidence includes a bot report but no explicit build command, objdiff output, or full-link log.

The PR body says a named data symbol gm_803DBFCC[] = "MvOpen.mth" was added, but the loaded diff excerpt for symbols.txt shows gm_803DBFCC changed to local @201 and the loaded source diff does not show the string declaration; this intake records the claim as PR-body evidence but does not promote it as independently verified source content.
Because raw_pr_json is truncated, this intake relies on the untruncated human_pr_text, raw_diff, changed_files, and text_corpus loaded files for exact evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Full-link small-data offsets can fail after marking a TU matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: When promoting a translation unit to Matching, verify externally referenced SDA symbols owned by that TU still land at the target final small-data offsets; a cross-object reference may resolve the expected symbol name but use a wrong offset if the owning TU's .sbss/.sdata layout shifts.
evidence_refs: pr_body: fn_801A1498 external SDA reference to gm_804D67EC had right symbol name but wrong final small-data offset
raw_diff: configure.py changed gmopening.c to Matching

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use declaration order and storage class to repair .sbss layout
accepted_candidate_records.text: For gmopening.c, matching the target .sbss layout required ordering gm_804D67EC, gm_804D67F0, and gm_804D67F4 correctly and making gm_804D67F4 non-static.
evidence_refs: pr_body: gm_804D67F4 needed to be non-static

source_update_candidates.title: gmopening.c owns gm_804D67EC small-data symbol
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2637 states that gm_804D67EC is owned by gmopening.c and that an external SDA reference from fn_801A1498 exposed a final small-data offset mismatch when gmopening.c was marked Matching.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-level fact for future full-link and small-data layout investigations; should be curated against current source/symbol ownership before acceptance.

source_update_candidates.title: Check cross-object SDA references before linking a matching object
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding guidance that Matching promotions should include narrow full-link or equivalent checks for externally referenced SDA symbols owned by the promoted object, because source declaration order and static/global visibility can affect final small-data offsets.
evidence_refs: pr_body: external SDA reference to gm_804D67EC caused full-link mismatch
source_update_candidates.reason: The case is concrete and aligns with existing data-sections-and-tu-splits and verification-ledger standards, but curator should decide whether it warrants a new note or remains covered by existing standards.

source_update_candidates.title: gmopening.c Matching promotion in PR #2637 depended on .sbss layout order
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2637 changed src/melee/gm/gmopening.c declarations so gm_804D67F4, gm_804D67F0, and gm_804D67EC layout matched target offsets, and changed gm_804D67F4 from static to non-static.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful local history for future edits around these declarations; should not be treated as a global rule without additional evidence.

rejection_notes: Do not infer from this PR alone that string literals should generally be promoted to named data symbols; the loaded diff does not clearly support the PR-body claim about adding gm_803DBFCC[] = "MvOpen.mth", and current standards caution against unsupported literal symbol promotion.
Do not infer reviewer intent or review consensus; the slice has zero review comments and zero reviews.
Do not claim an objdiff/checkdiff/build command was run; only the bot linked-code/data report is loaded.
